data.title: Spain's annual inflation rate eases to 2% in May
data.description:
• The Inflation rate in Spain decreased to 2% in May from 2.2% in April 2025. 

  • The Consumer Price Index in Spain increased 0.1% in May 2025 over the previous month.

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWP), (NASDAQ:EUFN)

  • Apollo highlights IMF warning on a trade war as investors navigate Trump’s tariff agenda
  • Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating on iShares MSCI Spain Capped ETF

data.title: France inflation drops to 0.7% in May
RH maintains 10–13% revenue growth outlook for 2025 as membership discount strategy targets market share gains
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: RH (RH) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Chairman & CEO Gary G. Friedman stated, "our industry-leading growth continued into fiscal 2025 as revenue increased 12% in the first quarter despite the polarizing impact of tariff uncertainty and the worst housing market in almost 50 years." He also highlighted that "adjusted operating margin of 7% and adjusted EBITDA of 13.1% were at the high end of our expectations, and we achieved positive free cash flow of $34 million in the quarter."

  • Friedman reported significant international momentum, citing RH England's gallery up 47% in demand and online demand at 44% for the quarter. He stated, "Current demand trends indicate the gallery will now reach approximately $37 million to $39 million of demand in 2025...with the online demand reaching approximately $8 million."

  • Expansion remains a focus, with plans for new galleries in Paris, London, and Milan, all in high-profile locations. Friedman expressed confidence: "When we assess the current business momentum...I can honestly say we have never been more excited or confident about the desirability of the RH brand globally."

  • A key strategic move was announced: "Another washtub bet is to play offense in the current environment by increasing our membership discount from 25% to 30%. This incremental incentive will position us to capture increased market share and drive additional membership."

  • On management changes, Friedman welcomed Lisa Chi back as President, Co-Chief Merchandising and Creative Officer, describing her as "a proven creative and merchandising force in our industry."

  • CFO Jack M. Preston stated, "our core business product margins were up year-over-year...and we expect it to be up year-over-year, the rest of the year."

Outlook

  • Friedman confirmed, "we are maintaining our current guidance for fiscal 2025, assuming the existing tariffs remain unchanged."

  • For fiscal 2025, management is forecasting revenue growth of 10% to 13%, adjusted operating margin of 14% to 15%, adjusted EBITDA margin of 20% to 21%, and free cash flow of $250 million to $350 million.

  • Second quarter 2025 guidance includes revenue growth of 8% to 10%, adjusted operating margin of 15% to 16%, and adjusted EBITDA margin of 20.5% to 21.5%.

  • Management noted a negative impact of approximately 6 points to Q2 revenue due to disruption from newly announced tariffs, expecting recovery in the second half.

Financial Results

  • Revenue increased 12% in the first quarter.

  • Adjusted operating margin reached 7% and adjusted EBITDA margin was 13.1%, both at the high end of expectations.

  • Free cash flow was $34 million for the quarter.

  • Inventory sequentially declined from $1.20 billion to $1.08 billion, a reduction of $12 million.

  • Preston explained, "the bigger move will be in the second half of the year as we keep going, yes, so that ability to meaningfully reduce inventory this year and next year."

Q&A

  • Steven Paul Forbes, Guggenheim: Asked about European demand forecasting and gallery performance. Friedman responded, "the RH brand as it is today, we believe we kind of have enough data to say it can be as disruptive and productive in Europe as it can be in America...if we can improve 3 pieces of this, being in stock, having the right fabrics...our team believes our business can double."

  • Simeon Ari Gutman, Morgan Stanley: Inquired about the impact of the limited-time outdoor sale and long-term discount strategy. Preston stated, "the 30% is a strategic move. It's not temporary. And our guidance is 20% to 21%."

  • Ariana Warden, Citi: Asked about second half sales confidence. Friedman explained, "our current performance, what we have in the pipeline, the number of new galleries we're opening, I mean there's a whole built-up model of pieces that says this is what it looks like."

  • Michael Lasser, UBS: Questioned discount profitability offset. Friedman said, "we generally will take a price change at the beginning of the year every year...we are coming out with some pretty big margin flexibility."

  • Maksim Rakhlenko, TD Cowen: Asked about inventory reduction and capital needs. Preston reported, "inventory was down just slightly from $1.20 billion to $1.08 billion, so down $12 million."

  • Cristina Fernández, Telsey Advisory: Inquired about tariff mitigation. Friedman emphasized, "we have tremendous partners that we've been working with for years. In a lot of ways, we operate like one company."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was probing and occasionally skeptical, especially regarding discounting strategies, inventory, and tariff disruptions, as reflected in questions about sustainability of margins and demand trajectory.

  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently pointing to strategic flexibility, competitive differentiation, and long-term growth. Friedman expressed, "we are more confident than less confident" and "we have the margin structure to be able to do things."

  • Compared to last quarter, analyst sentiment remained neutral to slightly cautious, while management’s confidence seemed to intensify, with more detailed articulation of strategic moves.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for revenue growth, operating margin, and EBITDA margin remains unchanged from the previous quarter.

  • The major new strategic announcement is the increase in membership discount from 25% to 30% as a permanent move, contrasted with last quarter’s more cautious stance on pricing.

  • The delay of a significant new concept launch from the second half of 2025 to spring 2026 was not previously communicated.

  • Analysts' focus shifted this quarter toward the sustainability of margin structure with the new discounting, tariff disruptions, and the timing of inventory reductions.

  • Management’s confidence and excitement about international expansion, particularly in Europe, was more pronounced than in the prior quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified ongoing risks from tariff uncertainty, inflation, and the housing market, with Friedman noting, "we expect a higher risk business environment this year due to the uncertainty caused by tariffs, market volatility, inflation risk and an increasing level of global discord."

  • The company is delaying the launch of a new brand extension to spring 2026 to mitigate tariff-related risks.

  • Management cited disruption from "Liberation Day" tariffs, leading to a projected negative impact on Q2 revenues but expects recovery in the back half.

  • Analysts also raised concerns over inventory levels and the potential need for capital, but management stated no current intention to raise capital at the current stock price.

Final Takeaway

Management underscored sustained growth and strategic momentum in the face of housing market headwinds and tariff disruptions, reaffirming guidance for double-digit revenue growth and robust margins. The permanent increase in membership discount to 30% signals an aggressive push for market share, while RH’s confidence in international expansion, particularly in Europe, stands out. Leadership maintains that investments and disciplined execution position the company for accelerated growth and improved cash flow as macro conditions recover.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Iran launches over 100 drones at Israel in retaliatory attack
data.description:
Iran has launched over 100 drones at Israel overnight in retaliation to Israel's "pre-emptive" strikes that targeted Iranian nuclear facilities and military sites late Thursday, heightening the risk of an all-out war in the Middle East.

"In the last few hours, Iran has launched more than 100 drones toward Israel, and all the defense systems are acting to intercept the threats," Effie Deffrin, Israeli military spokesman, said. The drones are expected to take several hours to reach Israel.

While the world suspected Israel would launch an attack on Iran, the severity took markets by surprise, with U.S. stock futures sliding more than 1% and oil prices surging 9%. 

The International Atomic Energy Agency confirmed that Iran's Natanz nuclear facility was hit, but no increase in radiation levels was observed at the site. The Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant and Isfahan nuclear site were not impacted, the IAEA added.

The attacks come as the U.S. and Iran were set to continue nuclear talks this weekend. "After these latest developments, it's not clear if they will still go ahead," ING analysts noted. "It is also worth mentioning that the U.S. has stated that it has had no involvement in the strikes against Iran. :

President Donald Trump on Thursday said a potential strike could very well happen. "As long as I think there is an agreement [with Iran], I don't want them [Israel] going in because I think that would blow it. Might help it actually, but it also could blow it."

The State Department on Wednesday "ordered the departure of non-emergency U.S. government personnel due to heightened regional tensions" in the Middle East. The U.S. has permanent military bases in Iraq, Saudi Arabia, Jordan, Kuwait, Egypt, Qatar, Bahrain, and the United Arab Emirates.

data.title: Dollarama prices $600 million offering of 3.850% senior unsecured notes due 2030
data.description:
• Dollarama (TSX:DOL:CA) has priced an offering of $600 million aggregate principal amount of 3.850% senior unsecured notes due December 16, 2030.
  • The Notes will be issued at par for aggregate gross proceeds of $600 million and will bear interest at a fixed rate of 3.850% per annum, payable semi-annually, until maturity on the 16th day of June and December of each year, commencing on December 16, 2025.
  • Dollarama (TSX:DOL:CA) intends to use the net proceeds to repay the $250 million aggregate principal amount of the corporation's outstanding 5.084% senior unsecured notes due October 27, 2025 which will be repaid in full at maturity, and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Nexon shares jump 10% after report of Tencent considering acquisition
data.description:
• Nexon (OTCPK:NEXOF) shares jumped 10% in Tokyo on Friday following a Bloomberg report that Tencent Holdings (OTCPK:TCEHY) (OTCPK:TCTZF) is exploring a potential acquisition of the gaming company to strengthen its position in the industry.
  • According to sources familiar with the situation, Tencent has approached the family of Nexon’s late founder, Kim Jung-ju, who controls their stake through the family investment firm NXC Corp., to discuss a possible deal.
  • Kim’s family has been speaking to advisers and evaluating options, the report said.

data.title: GameStop prices upsized private offering of $2.25B senior notes
Oklo prices capital raise of $400 via stock offering
Apple targets spring 2026 for release of delayed Siri AI upgrade - Bloomberg
data.description:
• Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) is said to have set an internal release target of spring 2026 for its delayed upgrade of Siri.
  • The company’s Siri team is aiming to bring the revamped voice assistant to market as part of an iOS 26.4 software update, Bloomberg reported.
  • The long-promised changes will allow Siri to tap into consumers’ personal data and on-screen activities to better fulfill queries.
  • Apple had originally planned to launch the new Siri in late 2024.

data.title: Germany inflation flat at 2.1% in May
data.description:
• Germany's inflation remained unchanged at 2.1% in May, meeting estimates.

  • The Consumer Price Index increased 0.10% in May over the previous month.

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR) (DAX:IND)
  • Currency: (EUR:USD)
